title: Fine-tune, RAG, or just prompt?
problem: You have 2,000 labelled customer support tickets (message + ideal agent reply). Your goal is to build a bot that replies in the exact tone and format your company uses. Decide: prompt engineering, RAG, or fine-tuning? Justify your choice and describe the training dataset format.
hints: Fine-tuning teaches style and format. RAG supplies facts. Prompting shapes a single call.
2,000 message->reply pairs is strong signal for tone — not for live facts like order status.
Fine-tune datasets are JSONL: one example per line, each a messages array.
solution: Fine-tune for tone and format — 2,000 clean input->output pairs directly encode how a good reply should sound. Use RAG alongside for live facts. Dataset format: JSONL, one example per line: {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "<ticket>"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<ideal reply>"}]}
